steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is 'Paul Austerlitz of Gettysburg College.' The phrase 'along with researchers Maureen Mahon of New York University and Michael Veal of Yale University' is a prepositional phrase, not a coordinating conjunction. It does not change the number of the subject. The verb must agree with the singular head noun 'Paul Austerlitz.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — are serving: Plural verb. Does not agree with the singular subject 'Paul Austerlitz.' ✗
  • Choice B — have served: Plural verb. Does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — serve: Plural verb (bare infinitive form used with plural or non-third-person subjects). Does not agree with the singular third-person subject. ✗
  • Choice D — serves: Singular third-person present-tense verb. Agrees with the singular subject 'Paul Austerlitz.' ✓
That confirms D is correct.
